reasoning: The question asks if reserve balances (WLODLL) will increase above their Jan 18 level (the Jan 14 value of 3.061M) by Jan 25 and Feb 17. The recent Jan 14 reading is a 3-month local peak. Since the Fed officially ended Quantitative Tightening on Dec 1, 2025, the structural downtrend has stopped, but short-term volatility is heavily driven by the Treasury General Account (TGA). The mid-January estimated tax deadline (Jan 15) transfers funds from depository institutions to the TGA, mechanically draining reserve balances in the short run. Therefore, the Jan 21 (covered by Jan 25) reading is highly likely to drop from the local peak. For the subsequent Feb 17 resolution, tax refund distributions begin to reverse this effect (draining the TGA and replenishing reserves), pulling the probability closer to 50%, though the high starting base makes an increase slightly less than a coin flip.
